Flying Dragons.description: Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown from the 1970s to the early 1990s. Formed in 1967 by immigrants primarily from Hong Kong, they were affiliated with the Hip Sing Tong. Engaged in extortion, kidnapping, murder, racketeering, illegal gambling, and heroin trafficking. The gang was largely dismantled after a 1994 federal indictment of 33 members.

Kurdistan Workers' Party.description: Kurdish militant political organization and armed guerrilla group primarily based in mountainous Kurdish-majority regions of southeastern Turkey, northern Iraq, and northeastern Syria. Founded on 27 November 1978 in Ziyaret, Lice, by Abdullah Öcalan and others. Initially sought an independent Kurdish state, later shifted to seeking autonomy and cultural rights within Turkey. Designated as a terrorist organization by Turkey, the US, EU, and others. Declared a ceasefire in March 2025 and announced plans for dissolution.

Bandidos Motorcycle Club.description: The Bandidos Motorcycle Club is a motorcycle gang classified as a criminal organization by law enforcement in multiple countries. In Denmark, the club has ten chapters and about 150 members involved in drug dealing, extortion, fraud, weapons trafficking, and violent crimes. The Danish Bandidos originated from the Morticians club, which rebranded as Undertakers and patched over by Bandidos in 1993. They were key participants in the Nordic Biker War (1994-1997) against the Hells Angels.

Vollaro clan.description: The Vollaro clan is a Neapolitan Camorra clan operating in the area east of Naples, specifically in Portici and San Sebastiano al Vesuvio. Founded in the mid-1970s by Luigi Vollaro, it engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, extortion, and other crimes. The clan fought internal wars and sided with the Alfieri clan against the Nuova Camorra Organizzata. Despite the arrest of its founder and subsequent leadership, the clan remains active.

Fourth Reich.description: The Fourth Reich was a racist skinhead prison gang formed in Paparua Prison in Christchurch, New Zealand, in the early 1990s. It terrorized communities in Nelson and the West Coast of the South Island, engaging in violent crimes with racist and bigoted dimensions. The gang was perceived as a credible terrorist threat during the early 2000s. There is little evidence that it still exists.

Lanzino.description: The Lanzino 'ndrina is a criminal clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cosenza, Calabria, Italy. It is allied with the Alvaro clan. The organization has been involved in extortion, public contract rigging, and vote-buying, with operations spanning from the 1980s to the present. Key members include Ettore Lanzino, who died in prison in 2024.

'Ndrina Bruzzise.description: The 'Ndrina Bruzzise is a 'Ndrangheta clan based in Palmi, Calabria, allied with the Parrello clan. It controlled the 'Montagna delle nevi' area and was involved in a long-running feud with the Gallico-Morgante-Sgrò-Sciglitano clan from 1977 to 1990. In the 2000s, the feud reignited, leading to the 2010 'Cosa mia' operation that arrested 46 members for extortion, mafia association, and infiltration in public contracts.

A.U.E..description: A.U.E. is an informal, well-hidden organization of Russian criminals, mainly consisting of children and teenagers, often indirectly ruled by adult criminals. It originated in Siberia and other parts of Russia, with roots in the 1980s but surfacing in the 2010s. The group engages in theft, murder, robbery, racketeering, extortion, and drug trafficking, primarily within educational and youth correction institutions. In 2020, the Supreme Court of Russia recognized it as an extremist movement and banned it.
